claim_statement: RSI + Bollinger Bands mean-reversion strategy is profitable
verdict_reason: no MCP-resolvable claims — strategy described verbally with no executable entry/exit rules
operationalization_notes: classified not testable at extraction — rules were descriptive not mechanical

claim_statement: RSI is overhyped and not consistently profitable as a standalone indicator
verdict_reason: opinion/market commentary — no falsifiable threshold or outcome
operationalization_notes: classified not testable at extraction — 'overhyped' is a take, not a claim

claim_statement: RSI divergence + EMA 200 strategy achieves 70% win rate across forex and gold
verdict_reason: no specific instrument named; '70% win rate across forex and gold' spans multiple symbols with no single testable entry
operationalization_notes: classified not testable — 'across forex and gold' is not operationalizable without a specific symbol

claim_statement: AlphaInsider platform signals outperform the market
verdict_reason: promotional claim — no specific entry/exit rule, no instrument, no testable performance threshold
operationalization_notes: promotion: 'outperform the market' with no mechanism or baseline definition

verdict_reason: trade walkthrough with no quantitative rule — describes one specific historical trade, not a repeatable testable claim
operationalization_notes: single-trade narrative; ORB 'acceptance' criterion not defined mechanically

claim_statement: ORB intraday breakout strategy (15-min range, 1:1.5R, exit by 3PM) works consistently on SPY
verdict_reason: no edge: profit factor 0.91, net profit -4082.41 over 66 trades
operationalization_notes: strategy compiled; 66 trades, PF 0.91, WR 48%, net -4082 | exits were explicit (3PM EOD) — strategy ran cleanly but had no edge at 1:1.5R

claim_statement: ICT key levels (order blocks, fair value gaps, liquidity) act as reliable support/resistance
verdict_reason: teaching a methodology — no specific price level, no specific instrument, no trigger condition that can be operationalized
operationalization_notes: ICT framework explanation: concepts described but no specific level named or quantified
